author: Scrog
text:
Generous anti-DoS backstops on plugin sync ingest

Three env-overridable, warn-not-fail backstops set far above any real payload, defending only against pathological sync abuse:

1. Absolute 64 MiB stream ceiling (BALLS_PLUGIN_ABS_MAX_STREAM_BYTES) that a raised BALLS_PLUGIN_MAX_STREAM_BYTES can never lift — closes the OOM hole where loosening the stream cap removed all protection.

2. Per-field truncation (BALLS_PLUGIN_MAX_SYNC_FIELD_BYTES, 1 MiB): an oversized title/description/note is clipped with a visible marker and a diagnostic; the rest of the sync still applies. external is not size-policed individually.

3. Create-flood ceiling (BALLS_PLUGIN_MAX_SYNC_CREATES, 5000): excess creates skipped with a warning, the rest applied.

Generous-but-real payloads pass byte-unchanged. README documents the knobs and philosophy. 100% coverage, clippy clean, files <300 lines.
